The first computer virus, 'Creeper,' was created in 1971 as an experiment.
The first computer mouse was made of wood by Doug Engelbart in 1964.
JavaScript was created in 10 days by Brendan Eich in 1995.
The term 'debugging' originated from a real moth found in a Harvard Mark II computer in 1947.
The first 1GB hard drive (1980) weighed 250kg and cost $40,000.
Python was named after Monty Python's Flying Circus, not the snake.
The first website (info.cern.ch) is still online today.
Google's first server was built with Lego bricks.
CAPTCHA stands for Completely Automated Public Turing test to tell Computers and Humans Apart.
The '@' symbol in email was chosen by Ray Tomlinson because it wasn't used in names.
Linux was created by Linus Torvalds in 1991 as a hobby project.
The first domain name ever registered was symbolics.com on March 15, 1985.
Amazon originally used a door as a desk in its early days.
The first YouTube video was titled 'Me at the zoo' uploaded on April 23, 2005.
Reddit was founded by two UVA graduates who originally wanted to create a mobile food ordering app.
The first text message was sent on December 3, 1992, saying 'Merry Christmas.'
The QWERTY keyboard layout was designed to prevent typewriter jams, not for speed.
The first computer programmer was Ada Lovelace in the 1840s.
HTTP 404 error code was named after room 404 at CERN where the World Wide Web team worked.
The first webcam was created to monitor a coffee pot at Cambridge University in 1991.
Java was originally called Oak, after a tree outside James Gosling's office.
The first smartphone, IBM Simon, was released in 1994.
The 'Save' icon uses a floppy disk that could hold only 1.44 MB.
The first computer game, Spacewar!, was created in 1962 at MIT.
SQL was originally called SEQUEL (Structured English Query Language).
The first computer in space was the Apollo Guidance Computer with 64KB of memory.
The world's first website is still hosted at CERN and viewable online today.
WiFi stands for Wireless Fidelity, coined as a marketing term in 1999.
The first microprocessor, Intel 4004, had 2,300 transistors. Modern CPUs have billions.
The '@' command in DOS was used to prevent a command from being displayed on screen.
The first laptop was the Osborne 1 (1981), weighing 11kg.
The word 'robot' comes from the Czech word 'robota' meaning forced labor.
Email existed before the internet — the first email was sent in 1971 on ARPANET.
The first computer password was created at MIT in 1961.
Google's original name was Backrub.
The first web browser, WorldWideWeb, was also a web editor.
The first banner ad on the web was for AT&T and had a 44% click-through rate.
PHP originally stood for Personal Home Page tools.
The first computer to beat a human at chess was IBM's Deep Blue in 1997.
The first SSD (Solid State Drive) was created in 1978 by StorageTek.
There are over 700 programming languages in existence today.
The first electronic programmable computer was the Colossus, built in 1943.
The inventor of C++, Bjarne Stroustrup, initially called it 'C with Classes.'
The first computer virus for MS-DOS was 'Brain,' created in 1986 by two Pakistani brothers.
Excel was originally created by Microsoft in 1985 for the Mac, not Windows.
The first commercial domain name was symbolics.com, registered in 1985.
The symbol # is called 'octothorpe' in formal contexts.
The first mobile phone call was made by Martin Cooper of Motorola in 1973.
JSON was created by Douglas Crockford in 2001, inspired by JavaScript object literals.
The first gigabyte hard drive weighed 250kg. A modern microSD card holds 1TB in 0.5g.
Stack Overflow was founded in 2008 by Jeff Atwood and Joel Spolsky.
The first camera phone was the Sharp J-SH04, released in 2000 in Japan.
The first hard drive was IBM's RAMAC 305 in 1956, storing 5MB and weighing over a ton.
The most starred GitHub repo is freeCodeCamp with over 400k stars.
DNS stands for Domain Name System, created in 1983 by Paul Mockapetris.
The first emoji was created by Shigetaka Kurita in 1999 for NTT DoCoMo.
AI researcher Geoffrey Hinton published the backpropagation paper in 1986.
The first commercial website was created by Tim Berners-Lee in 1991.
React was created by Jordan Walke, a software engineer at Meta.
Docker was released in 2013 by Solomon Hykes as an internal project at dotCloud.
The first known computer bug was a moth found in the Harvard Mark II relay in 1947.
Linux runs on more than 90% of the world's supercomputers.
The first Google index had 24 million pages in 1999.
Git was created by Linus Torvalds in 2005 to manage the Linux kernel.
The first 1TB hard drive was released by Hitachi in 2007.
TypeScript was created by Anders Hejlsberg at Microsoft, first released in 2012.
The most expensive domain ever sold is voice.com at $30 million in 2019.
AI model training can produce as much CO2 as 5 cars over their lifetime.
The first floppy disk was 8 inches in diameter and could hold 80KB.
The concept of 'cloud computing' dates back to the 1960s with J.C.R. Licklider.
The average person spends about 6.5 years of their life on the internet.
There are currently over 1.9 billion websites on the World Wide Web.
The first search engine was Archie, created in 1990 by Alan Emtage.
SSL/TLS encryption was developed by Netscape in 1994.
The first MP3 player, the MPMan, was released in 1998 with 32MB of storage.
The first camera with a digital sensor was built by Kodak engineer Steve Sasson in 1975.
Java runs on over 3 billion devices worldwide.
The first wearable computer was a shoe-based device created by MIT students in 1996.
The term 'API' (Application Programming Interface) dates back to the 1960s.
The first computer with a GUI was the Xerox Alto in 1973.
The world's first website was created at CERN in Switzerland.
Google's first tweet in 2009 was 'I'm feeling lucky' in binary.
The first Instagram post was a photo of a pier in Mexico by co-founder Kevin Systrom.
The term 'spam' for unwanted emails comes from a Monty Python sketch.
The first spam email was sent in 1978 by a Digital Equipment Corporation marketer.
Node.js was created by Ryan Dahl in 2009, originally using the SpiderMonkey engine.
The most popular programming language on GitHub is JavaScript.
The first VR headset was the Sensorama in 1962, created by Morton Heilig.
The term 'open source' was coined by Christine Peterson in 1998.
The first computer to play Go at professional level was AlphaGo in 2016.
The .com TLD was created in 1985, originally managed by the US Department of Defense.
The first synthetic voice was created by Homer Dudley in 1939 at Bell Labs.
Zoom was created by Eric Yuan, a former WebEx engineer, in 2011.
The first Apple computer was built in Steve Jobs' garage in 1976.
The term 'viral' for internet content was first used in 1997.
The first tweet was sent by Jack Dorsey on March 21, 2006.
The first CD-ROM drive for computers was released by Philips in 1985.
The .io domain actually belongs to the British Indian Ocean Territory.
The first USB drive was invented by IBM in 1998, holding 8MB.
Slack was originally developed as an internal tool for a gaming company.
The first commercial antivirus software was released in 1987 by G Data Software.
The 'like' button on Facebook was created in 2009 and was originally called 'awesome.'
The first Amazon Web Services (AWS) product was SQS in 2004.
The term 'bit' is short for 'binary digit,' coined by John Tukey in 1946.
The first 3D-printed car was the Strati, produced in 2014.
The first web server was a NeXTcube workstation used by Tim Berners-Lee.
Minecraft was created by Markus Persson in 2009 as a single weekend project.
The first commercial website to accept credit cards was Pizza Hut in 1994.
The first RAM was magnetic core memory, invented in 1951 by Jay Forrester.
The word 'software' was first used in print by John Tukey in 1958.
The first graphic design software was MacPaint, released in 1984.
The first internet domain registered in Africa was .za in 1990.
CSS was first proposed by Håkon Wium Lie in 1994 at CERN.
The first commercial SSD had a capacity of 45MB and cost over $100,000.
The term 'Web 2.0' was popularized by Tim O'Reilly in 2004.
The first HTML specification was published by Tim Berners-Lee in 1991.
The first robot to walk on two legs was WABOT-1 in 1973 in Japan.
The first gigabit internet connection was achieved in 2005.
The first neural network was the Perceptron, created by Frank Rosenblatt in 1958.
The term 'metaverse' was coined by Neal Stephenson in his 1992 novel Snow Crash.
The first web API was the Salesforce API, launched in 2000.
The first computer with 1GB of RAM was the IBM PS/2 Model 70 in 1989.
The first fiber optic cable was laid across the Atlantic Ocean in 1988.
The first LCD screen was invented by George Heilmeier in 1964 at RCA.
The first commercial website builder was GeoCities, launched in 1994.
The first smartwatch was the Pulsar, released in 1972.
The first computer algorithm was written by Ada Lovelace for the Analytical Engine.
The first drone was developed in 1915 by Nikola Tesla.
The first OLED display was invented by Ching Tang at Kodak in 1987.
The term 'big data' was first used in 1997 by NASA researchers.
The first AI chatbot was ELIZA, created by Joseph Weizenbaum in 1966.
The first synthetic biology circuit was created in 2000.
The first electric car with lithium-ion batteries was the Tesla Roadster in 2008.
The first quantum computer was proposed by Richard Feynman in 1981.
The first 3D-printed human organ transplant was a bladder in 2013.
The first bitcoin transaction was between Satoshi Nakamoto and Hal Finney in 2009.
The first blockchain was conceptualized by Satoshi Nakamoto in 2008.
The first NFT was created in 2014 by Kevin McCoy on the Namecoin blockchain.
The first smart contract platform was Ethereum, launched in 2015.
The first generative AI model, GPT-1, was released by OpenAI in 2018.
The first self-driving car was the NavLab in 1986 at Carnegie Mellon.
The first public blockchain was Bitcoin, launched in January 2009.
The first DAO (Decentralized Autonomous Organization) was created in 2016.
The first web browser with tabs was InternetWorks in 1994.
The first CSS framework was Blueprint CSS, released in 2007.
The first JavaScript library was jQuery, released by John Resig in 2006.
The first responsive website was designed by Ethan Marcotte in 2010.
The first CMS (Content Management System) was Vignette StoryServer in 1995.
The first version control system was SCCS, created at Bell Labs in 1972.
The first GUI operating system for IBM PCs was Windows 1.0 in 1985.
The first UNIX system was created at Bell Labs in 1969 by Ken Thompson and Dennis Ritchie.
The first computer with a mouse was the Xerox Star in 1981.
The first laptop with a color screen was the IBM ThinkPad in 1994.
The first tablet computer was the GRiDPad in 1989.
The first smart TV was released by LG in 2007.
The first fitness tracker was the Fitbit Tracker in 2009.
The first wireless charger was introduced by Palm in 2009.
The first solar-powered calculator was the Teal Photon in 1978.
The first autocorrect system was developed for the Apple Newton in 1992.
The first speech recognition system was IBM Shoebox in 1961.
The first optical mouse was invented by Steve Kirsch at MIT in 1980.
The first wireless mouse was released by Logitech in 1991.
The first mechanical keyboard switch was the Cherry MX in 1985.
The first computer monitor was the IBM 2250 in 1964.
The first digital camera was the Kodak DCS-100 in 1991.
The first video call was made by AT&T in 1964.
The first streaming service was RealAudio in 1995.
The first podcast was created by Adam Curry in 2004.
The first e-book reader was the Rocket eBook in 1998.
The first facial recognition system was developed in 1964 by Woodrow Wilson Bledsoe.
The first augmented reality system was the Sword of Damocles in 1968.
The first online payment system was PayPal, founded in 1998.
The first online marketplace was eBay, founded in 1995.
The first social network was SixDegrees.com in 1997.
The first photo-sharing app was Flickr in 2004.
The first video-sharing platform was YouTube in 2005.
The first music-streaming service was Napster in 1999.
The first wiki was WikiWikiWeb, created by Ward Cunningham in 1995.
The first blog was Links.net, created by Justin Hall in 1994.
The first meme was the dancing baby animation, popular in 1996.
The first emoticon was :-) proposed by Scott Fahlman in 1982.
The first online game was MUD (Multi-User Dungeon) in 1978.
The first MMORPG was Meridian 59, launched in 1996.
The first eSports tournament was the Space Invaders Championship in 1980.
The first computer-animated film was Toy Story in 1995.
The first CGI character in a film was the stained-glass knight in Young Sherlock Holmes (1985).
The first digital painting program was Sketchpad, created by Ivan Sutherland in 1963.
The first 3D modeling software was Sketchpad, also created in 1963.
The first image file format was BMP, introduced with Windows in 1985.
The first lossy compression format was JPEG, standardized in 1992.
The first lossless audio format was FLAC, released in 2001.
The first video codec was H.261, standardized in 1988.
The first DVD player was released in Japan in 1996.
The first Blu-ray player was released by Sony in 2006.
The first VCR was the Sony Betamax in 1975.
The first laser printer was the IBM 3800 in 1976.
The first inkjet printer was the HP ThinkJet in 1984.
The first 3D printer was the SLA-1, created by Chuck Hull in 1983.
The first barcode scanner was installed in a grocery store in 1974.
The first QR code was created by Denso Wave in 1994 for tracking auto parts.
The first RFID tag was developed in 1973 by Mario Cardullo.
The first NFC-enabled phone was the Nokia 6131 in 2006.
The first fingerprint scanner on a phone was the Toshiba G500 in 2007.
The first phone with a touchscreen was the IBM Simon in 1992.
The first iPhone was released on June 29, 2007.
The first Android phone was the HTC Dream (G1) in 2008.
The first Samsung Galaxy phone was released in 2009.
The first phone with a built-in camera was the Kyocera VP-210 in 1999.
The first 5G phone call was made by Samsung and Verizon in 2019.
The first GPS-enabled phone was the Benefon Esc! in 1999.
The first phone with a color screen was the Siemens S10 in 1997.
The first phone with a QWERTY keyboard was the Nokia 9000 Communicator in 1996.
The first BlackBerry phone was the 850 in 1999.
The first wearable phone was the Samsung Watch Phone in 1999.
The first foldable phone was the Royole FlexPai in 2018.
The first satellite phone call was made from Motorola's Iridium system in 1998.
The first transatlantic phone cable (TAT-1) was laid in 1956.
The first fiber optic phone call was made in 1977.
The first VoIP call was made in 1995 by VocalTec.
The first 911 call was made on February 16, 1968 in Haleyville, Alabama.
The first mobile phone with internet was the Nokia 7110 in 1999.
The first WAP browser was on the Nokia 7110 in 1999.
The first web page on mobile was created in 1999.
The first mobile app store was Handango, launched in 2000.
The first Apple App Store had 500 apps at launch in 2008.
The first Google Play Store (then Android Market) had 50 apps in 2008.
The first in-app purchase was in a game called Tap Tap Revenge in 2008.
The first mobile game was Tetris on the Hagenuk MT-2000 in 1994.
The first Angry Birds game was released in 2009 for iOS.
The first Candy Crush game was released in 2012 on Facebook.
The first Pokemon Go was released in July 2016.
The first Fortnite season started in July 2017.
The first Minecraft version was released in May 2009.
The first esports game was StarCraft in 1998.
The first video game console was the Magnavox Odyssey in 1972.
The first handheld game console was the Microvision in 1979.
The first Nintendo console was the Color TV-Game in 1977.
The first PlayStation was released in 1994 in Japan.
The first Xbox was released in 2001 in North America.
The first Wii was released in 2006.
The first game with a save feature was The Legend of Zelda in 1986.
The first Easter egg in a video game was in Adventure (1979) for Atari 2600.
The first DLC (Downloadable Content) was for the game Total Annihilation in 1997.
The first game with microtransactions was the 2005 game The Movies.
The first loot box mechanic was in the 2007 game Team Fortress 2.
The first battle royale game was H1Z1, released in 2015.
The first FPS game was Wolfenstein 3D in 1992.
The first RTS game was Dune II in 1992.
The first MMORPG to reach 1M subscribers was Ultima Online in 1997.
The first game with full voice chat was Xbox Live in 2002.

The first game streaming service was OnLive in 2010.
The first VR headset for gaming was Oculus Rift in 2016.
The first AR game was Pokémon GO in 2016.
The first game to use ray tracing was Quake II RTX in 2019.
The first game to use DLSS was Battlefield V in 2019.

The first modem was the Hayes Smartmodem in 1981.
The first DSL connection was achieved in 1984.
The first cable modem was used in 1995.
The first satellite internet was launched in 1996.
The first Wi-Fi standard (802.11) was released in 1997.
The first Bluetooth device was a headset released by Ericsson in 2000.
The first USB standard was released in 1996 by Intel.
The first FireWire standard was released by Apple in 1995.
The first Thunderbolt standard was released by Intel in 2011.
The first HDMI standard was released in 2002.
The first DisplayPort standard was released in 2006.
The first VGA standard was released by IBM in 1987.
The first DVI standard was released in 1999.
The first computer with built-in Wi-Fi was the iBook in 1999.
The first computer with Bluetooth was the iMac G4 in 2002.
The first computer with USB was the iMac G3 in 1998.
The first computer with FireWire was the Power Mac G3 in 1999.
The first computer with Thunderbolt was the MacBook Pro in 2011.
The first computer with HDMI was the HP Pavilion in 2004.
The first computer with SSD was the MacBook Air in 2008.
The first computer with a solid-state drive was the Sony Vaio U8 in 2004.
The first computer with a multi-core processor was the IBM POWER4 in 2001.
The first computer with a 64-bit processor was the AMD Opteron in 2003.
The first computer with a 1GHz processor was the AMD Athlon in 2000.
The first computer with a 100MHz processor was the Intel Pentium in 1993.
The first computer to run at 1GHz was the IBM RS/6000 SP in 1999.
The first computer with 1GB of storage was the IBM 3340 in 1973.
The first computer with 1TB of storage was the HP ProLiant in 2006.
The first computer to render a 3D scene was the University of Utah in 1972.
The first computer to recognize speech was IBM's Shoebox in 1961.
The first computer to translate languages was IBM's Georgetown-IBM experiment in 1954.
The first computer to beat a world champion in chess was IBM's Deep Blue in 1997.
The first computer to pass the Turing Test was the chatbot Eugene Goostman in 2014.
The first computer to write a book was the Novel Writer program in 1973.
The first computer to compose music was the Illiac Suite in 1957.
The first computer to paint a picture was the AARON program by Harold Cohen in 1973.
The first computer-generated film was 'Hummingbird' in 1972.
The first computer to predict protein folding was AlphaFold, released by DeepMind in 2020.
The first generative pre-trained transformer (GPT) was GPT-1 by OpenAI in 2018 with 117M parameters.
The first search engine to use page ranking was Google's PageRank algorithm in 1998.
The first distributed version control system was BitKeeper in 1998.
The first open-source license was the GNU General Public License (GPL) in 1989.
The first hacker conference was DEF CON in 1993, founded by Jeff Moss.
The first digital signature was invented by Diffie and Hellman in 1976.
The first public key cryptography system was RSA, invented in 1977 by Rivest, Shamir, and Adleman.
